feed.title: The future of the Bitcoin Core GUI

entry.title: The future of the Bitcoin Core GUI
entry.summary:
In an innovative development aimed at enhancing the usability and accessibility of Bitcoin's RPC API, a new approach has been adopted to describe this interface in a more structured manner. By revisiting and refining the ideas initially put forward in [bitcoin Issue29912](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/issues/29912), a significant advancement has been made through the creation of an OpenRPC spec file. This specification, which can be found in its latest iteration [here](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/willcl-ark/bitcoin/8eb8112c66d5d79158394ba3f6677473c57f7d87/doc/openrpc.json), serves as a foundational element for further developments.
Building on this groundwork, a remarkable tool was swiftly developed: a dynamic webkit-based "RPC GUI". The creation of this graphical user interface was facilitated by the OpenRPC spec, demonstrating the practical benefits of having a formal, standardized description of the Bitcoin RPC API. This GUI represents a leap forward in making Bitcoin's RPC functionalities more accessible to users and developers alike, requiring only about 20 minutes to develop thanks to the clarity and structure provided by the OpenRPC spec.
Further experimentation and exploration of the possibilities inherent in the RPC API have led to the creation of a basic yet promising [bitcoin-tui](https://github.com/janb84/bitcoin-tui). This textual user interface (TUI) stands as a testament to what can be achieved with direct calls to the RPC, offering a glimpse into the potential for innovative tools and applications that can enhance the user experience and broaden the utility of Bitcoin's underlying technologies. These developments underscore the value of clear, structured API specifications in fostering innovation and facilitating the creation of user-friendly tools that can bring complex technologies closer to a broader audience.
